curriculum.subject: Physics
curriculum.gradeLevel: Class XI / Class XII / 12th Grade / Science
curriculum.overallDescription: 19 units, 28 chapters, and 0 topics extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Understand the need for measurement and distinguish between fundamental and derived units within various systems of units.
Apply SI units correctly using standard symbols and nomenclature as per international standards.
Determine appropriate significant figures in calculations involving physical quantities.
Calculate uncertainty in results based on measured values.
Perform dimensional analysis to derive relationships between physical quantities and verify the correctness of equations.
Define frame of reference and describe motion along a straight line.
Apply elementary concepts of differentiation and integration to derive velocity and position functions from acceleration or vice versa.
Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity in uniform and non-uniform motions.
Analyze velocity-time and position-time graphs to interpret the nature of motion (uniformly accelerated).
Derive and apply relations for uniformly accelerated motion using both graphical methods and calculus.
Differentiate between scalar and vector quantities and perform operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication) on vectors including dot and cross products.
Resolve a vector into rectangular components in a plane using unit vectors.

To determine mass of a given body using a metre scale by principle of moments.

To measure the force of limiting friction for rolling of a roller on a horizontal plane.
To study the variation in range of a projectile with angle of projection.
To study the conservation of energy of a ball rolling down on an inclined plane (using a double inclined plane).
To study dissipation of energy of a simple pendulum by plotting a graph between square of amplitude and time.
To observe change of state and plot a cooling curve for molten wax.
To observe and explain the effect of heating on a bi-metallic strip.

To study the effect of detergent on surface tension of water by observing capillary rise.

To measure resistance, voltage (AC/DC), current (AC) and check continuity of a given circuit using multimeter.
To assemble a household circuit comprising three bulbs, three (on/off) switches, a fuse and a power source.

To study the variation in potential drop with length of a wire for a steady current.

To draw the diagram of a given open circuit comprising at least a battery, resistor/rheostat, key, ammeter and voltmeter. Mark the components that are not connected in proper order and correct the circuit and also the circuit diagram.

competency_groups.title: Physical World and Measurement - Units and Measurements
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to measurement systems, SI units, significant figures, dimensional analysis, and uncertainty determination.
competencies: Understand the need for measurement and distinguish between fundamental and derived units within various systems of units.
Apply SI units correctly using standard symbols and nomenclature as per international standards.
Determine appropriate significant figures in calculations involving physical quantities.
Calculate uncertainty in results based on measured values.
Perform dimensional analysis to derive relationships between physical quantities and verify the correctness of equations.

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Straight Line
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to describing motion using calculus, analyzing graphs of uniform/non-uniform motion, and understanding kinematic equations.
competencies: Define frame of reference and describe motion along a straight line.
Apply elementary concepts of differentiation and integration to derive velocity and position functions from acceleration or vice versa.
Distinguish between average speed/velocity and instantaneous velocity in uniform and non-uniform motions.
Analyze velocity-time and position-time graphs to interpret the nature of motion (uniformly accelerated).
Derive and apply relations for uniformly accelerated motion using both graphical methods and calculus.

competency_groups.title: Kinematics - Motion in a Plane
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to vector algebra, resolution of vectors, and analysis of projectile and circular motion.
competencies: Differentiate between scalar and vector quantities and perform operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication) on vectors including dot and cross products.
Resolve a vector into rectangular components in a plane using unit vectors.
Analyze cases of uniform velocity and uniform acceleration for motion in two dimensions.
Derive equations of motion for projectile motion and calculate range, height, and time of flight.
Describe the dynamics of uniform circular motion including centripetal force.

competency_groups.title: Laws of Motion - Dynamics and Friction
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Newton's laws, conservation of momentum, equilibrium conditions, and frictional forces.
competencies: Explain the intuitive concept of force, inertia, and state Newton's three laws of motion.
Apply the law of conservation of linear momentum to solve collision problems.
Analyze equilibrium conditions for concurrent forces including static and kinetic friction.
Derive expressions for rolling friction and explain lubrication effects qualitatively.
Calculate centripetal force required for circular motion in scenarios like vehicles on level or banked roads.

competency_groups.title: Work, Energy and Power - Conservation Principles
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to work done by forces, energy theorems, potential energy concepts, and collision analysis.
competencies: Calculate work done by constant and variable forces using integration or graphical methods.
Apply the Work-Energy theorem to relate net work to change in kinetic energy.
Distinguish between conservative (e.g., spring) and non-conservative forces based on potential energy concepts.
Analyze motion in a vertical circle considering tension and gravity variations.
Classify collisions as elastic or inelastic and apply conservation laws for one-dimensional and two-dimensional impacts.

competency_groups.title: System of Particles and Rigid Body - Rotational Dynamics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to center of mass motion, torque, angular momentum conservation, and rotational kinematics.
competencies: Determine the center of mass for two-particle systems and rigid bodies (e.g., uniform rod).
Apply conservation of linear momentum in relation to center of mass motion.
Calculate moment of force (torque) and angular momentum, applying the law of conservation of angular momentum.
Analyze equilibrium conditions for rigid bodies under rotational forces.
Compare linear and rotational motions using equations of rotational motion and concepts like moment of inertia.
competency_groups.unit_name: Motion of System of Particles and Rigid Body
competency_groups.chapter_name: System of Particles and Rotational Motion

competency_groups.title: Gravitation - Planetary and Satellite Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to gravitational laws, potential energy variations, and satellite dynamics.
competencies: State Kepler's laws of planetary motion and the universal law of gravitation.
Analyze variation in acceleration due to gravity with altitude and depth from Earth's surface.
Calculate gravitational potential energy and potential for various mass distributions.
Derive expressions for escape speed and orbital velocity of satellites.
Determine total mechanical energy of an orbiting satellite.

competency_groups.title: Properties of Bulk Matter - Solids and Elasticity
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to stress-strain relationships, elastic moduli, and material properties.
competencies: Define elasticity and explain the stress-strain relationship including Hooke's law.
Calculate Young’s modulus, bulk modulus, shear modulus (qualitative), and Poisson's ratio for materials.
Determine elastic energy stored in deformed bodies.
Apply qualitative concepts of elastic behavior to real-world material applications.

competency_groups.title: Properties of Bulk Matter - Fluids and Surface Tension
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to fluid statics, dynamics (Bernoulli's theorem), viscosity, and surface phenomena.
competencies: Apply Pascal's law to hydraulic systems and calculate pressure due to fluid columns.
Analyze viscous flow using Stokes' law, terminal velocity, and Reynolds number concepts (streamline vs turbulent).
Derive Bernoulli's theorem and apply it to Torricelli's law and dynamic lift scenarios.
Explain surface tension phenomena including capillary rise, excess pressure in drops/bubbles, and angle of contact.

competency_groups.title: Properties of Bulk Matter - Thermal Expansion and Heat Transfer
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to thermal expansion, specific heat, calorimetry, and modes of heat transfer.
competencies: Quantify thermal expansion in solids, liquids, and gases including anomalous expansion of water.
Calculate specific heat capacity (Cp, Cv) and apply calorimetry principles for latent heat calculations.
Distinguish between conduction, convection, and radiation as modes of heat transfer.
Apply qualitative concepts of Blackbody radiation, Wien's displacement law, and Stefan's law.

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamics - Laws and Processes
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to thermodynamic laws, state variables, and analysis of gas processes.
competencies: Define temperature using the zeroth law and distinguish between heat, work, and internal energy (first law).
Apply the second law of thermodynamics regarding entropy and directionality of processes.
Analyze gaseous state changes including isothermal, adiabatic, reversible, irreversible, and cyclic processes.

competency_groups.title: Kinetic Theory of Gases - Molecular Behavior
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the kinetic theory assumptions, gas laws derived from molecular motion, and statistical concepts.
competencies: Derive the equation of state for a perfect gas using kinetic theory assumptions.
Interpret temperature as a measure of average kinetic energy (rms speed) of molecules.
Apply the law of equipartition of energy to specific heat capacities of gases.
Explain concepts of mean free path and Avogadro's number in the context of gas behavior.
competency_groups.unit_name: Behaviour of Perfect Gases and Kinetic Theory of Gases

competency_groups.title: Oscillations - Simple Harmonic Motion
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to periodic motion, SHM equations, energy in oscillation, and pendulum dynamics.
competencies: Distinguish between general periodic functions and simple harmonic motion (SHM).
Derive the equation of motion for a loaded spring and analyze restoring forces.
Calculate kinetic and potential energies during SHM and determine total energy conservation.
Derive the time period expression for a simple pendulum.

competency_groups.title: Waves - Propagation, Superposition, and Interference
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to wave types, superposition principle, standing waves, beats, and diffraction.
competencies: Differentiate between transverse and longitudinal waves and describe their propagation speed.
Apply the principle of superposition to analyze interference patterns (Young's double slit).
Analyze reflection phenomena leading to standing waves in strings and organ pipes, identifying fundamental modes and harmonics.
Explain the phenomenon of beats resulting from the superposition of two close frequencies.

competency_groups.title: Electrostatics - Charges, Fields, and Flux
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Coulomb's law, electric fields, dipoles, Gauss's theorem, and flux calculations.
competencies: Apply the principle of superposition and conservation of charge in systems with multiple point charges.
Calculate electric field intensity due to point charges, continuous distributions, and dipoles (axial/equatorial).
Determine torque on an electric dipole placed in a uniform electric field.
Apply Gauss's theorem to calculate fields for infinite wires, plane sheets, and spherical shells.

competency_groups.title: Electrostatic Potential - Energy, Capacitance, and Dielectrics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to electric potential, equipotential surfaces, energy storage in capacitors, and dielectric effects.
competencies: Calculate electric potential and potential difference due to point charges, dipoles, and charge systems.
Identify properties of conductors, insulators, free/bound charges, and dielectrics (polarization).
Analyze capacitors in series/parallel combinations and calculate capacitance with/without dielectric media.
Determine energy stored in a capacitor using standard formulae.

competency_groups.title: Current Electricity - Circuits, Ohm's Law, and Networks
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to drift velocity, resistivity, Kirchhoff's laws, Wheatstone bridge, and cell combinations.
competencies: Relate electric current to drift velocity and mobility of charge carriers in conductors.
Analyze V-I characteristics (linear/non-linear) and temperature dependence of resistance/resistivity/conductivity.
Apply Kirchhoff's rules to solve complex circuit networks including Wheatstone bridge configurations.
Calculate equivalent EMF, internal resistance, and terminal potential difference for cells in series/parallel.

competency_groups.title: Magnetic Effects - Fields, Forces, and Instruments
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Biot-Savart law, Ampere's law, magnetic forces on charges/conductors, and galvanometer applications.
competencies: Apply Biot-Savart law for circular loops and Ampere's law for straight wires/solenoids (qualitative).
Calculate force on moving charges in combined electric/magnetic fields and current-carrying conductors.
Analyze torque on a current loop, defining magnetic dipole moment and its relation to galvanometer sensitivity.
Design conversions of galvanometers into ammeters or voltmeters.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Magnetism - Materials, Dipoles, and Properties
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to magnetic dipoles, properties of materials (para/dia/ferro), and temperature effects.
competencies: Model a bar magnet as an equivalent solenoid qualitatively.
Calculate torque on a magnetic dipole in a uniform field and describe magnetic field lines.
Classify substances based on magnetic properties (para-, dia-, ferro-) with examples.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-III Magnetic Effects of Current and Magnetism

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Induction - Laws and Applications
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to Faraday's laws, Lenz's law, self/mutual induction, and induced EMF.
competencies: State and apply Faraday's laws of electromagnetic induction to calculate induced EMF.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Alternating Current - AC Circuits, Power, and Transformers
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to RMS values, reactance/impedance, resonance in LCR circuits, power factor, and transformer operation.
competencies: Distinguish between peak and RMS values of alternating current/voltage.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit-IV Electromagnetic Induction and Alternating Currents

competency_groups.title: Electromagnetic Waves - Spectrum and Properties
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to displacement current, wave characteristics, transverse nature, and the electromagnetic spectrum.
competencies: Explain the concept of displacement current leading to the existence of EM waves.

competency_groups.title: Ray Optics - Reflection, Refraction, and Instruments
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to mirror/lens formulas, total internal reflection, optical fibers, and magnifying powers of instruments.
competencies: Apply the mirror formula and lens maker's formula for spherical mirrors and thin lenses.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–9: Ray Optics and Optical Instruments

competency_groups.title: Wave Optics - Interference, Diffraction, and Huygens' Principle
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to wave fronts, interference patterns (Young's experiment), diffraction limits, and coherence.
competencies: Apply Huygen's principle to derive laws of reflection and refraction.

competency_groups.title: Dual Nature - Photoelectric Effect and Matter Waves
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to particle nature of light, photoelectric equation, de-Broglie relation, and wave-particle duality.
competencies: Explain the experimental observations (Hertz/Lenard) leading to Einstein's photoelectric equation.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit–VII Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–11: Dual Nature of Radiation and Matter

competency_groups.title: Atoms - Rutherford, Bohr Models, and Spectra
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to atomic structure models, quantization of orbits, energy levels, and hydrogen spectra.
competencies: Describe the alpha-particle scattering experiment leading to Rutherford's nuclear model.

competency_groups.title: Nuclei - Structure, Binding Energy, Fission/Fusion
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to nuclear composition, mass-energy equivalence, binding energy curves, and nuclear reactions.
competencies: Explain the concept of mass defect and its relation to binding energy per nucleon.

competency_groups.title: Electronic Devices - Semiconductors, Diodes, and Rectification
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to energy bands, intrinsic/extrinsic semiconductors, p-n junction characteristics, and diode applications.
competencies: Differentiate between conductors, insulators, and semiconductors based on energy band theory.
competency_groups.chapter_name: Chapter–14: Semiconductor Electronics: Materials, Devices and Simple Circuits
